cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure this out. So, the problem is asking between what times Thomas could have gone to the football field. The key here is to look at the alibis or the times when Thomas was seen doing other things, and find a time when he was not accounted for. Also, we have to consider that the football field is closed after 9pm, so he must have gone before that. First, let me list out all the times when Thomas was seen by other people: 1. Thomas woke up at 8am. That's just a start time, but not an activity that takes time. 2. David saw Thomas fixing their computer at the electronic store from 8am to 10am. So between 8 and 10, he was at the electronic store. 3. Jennifer saw him buying lunch at the deli from 10am to 12pm. So 10 to 12, he was at the deli. 4. Lisa saw him walking towards the Statue of Liberty from 12pm to 2pm. So 12 to 2, he was walking towards the statue. 5. Sarah saw him buying a phone at the electronics store from 2pm to 5pm. 2 to 5, he was at the electronics store again. 6. Kimberly saw him buying a bike at the bike shop from 6pm to 9pm. 6 to 9, he was at the bike shop. 7. The football field was closed after 9pm, so he must have gone before that. Now, let's look for gaps between these times. Let's go in order: - Thomas woke up at 8am. But the first activity is from 8am to 10am (electronic store). So from 8 to 10, he's occupied.
